person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com

Marguerite Beatrice of Savoy (of Geneva)
French: Béatrice-Marguerite de Savoie (de Genève), Italian: Beatrice-Margherita di Savoia (di Ginevra), Spanish: Beatriz Margarita de Savoya (de Ginebra)
Also Known As:	"Marguerite Beatrix de Genève"
Birthdate:	circa 1180
Birthplace:	Geneva, Switzerland
Death:	April 08, 1257 (72-81)
Pierre Chatel, Isere, Rhone-Alpes, France 
Place of Burial:	Saint-Pierre-de-Curtville, Savoy, France
Immediate Family:	
Daughter of Guillaume I, comte de Genève and Béatrice Marguerite de Faucigny
Wife of Thomas I, count of Savoy
Fiancée of Philip II Augustus, king of France

Mother of 
Amadeus IV, count of Savoy; 
Humbert comte de Savoie; 
Thomas II, count-regent of Savoy;
 Aymon Lord of Chablais; 
Wiliam of Savoy, bishop of Valence; 
Peter II, count of Savoy "the Little Charlemagne";
 Boniface de Savoie; 
Béatrice de Savoie, comtesse consort de Provence;
 Philip I, count of Savoy; 
Alix de Savoie; 
Marguerite de Savoie; 
NN de Savoie; 
NN de Savoie and 
Agathe de Savoie 

Sister of Aimon de Genève; Amédée de Genève, évêque de Maurienne; Guillaume II, comte de Genève and Agathe de Genève
Half sister of Humbert, comte de Genève


Occupation:	countess consort of Savoy

Margaret of Geneva (1180?-1252), countess of Savoy, was the daughter of William I, Count of Geneva, and Beatrice de Faucigny (1160-1196).

She was supposed to become the third wife of Philip II of France. However, when her father was escorting her to France in May 1195, Thomas I of Savoy carried her off. Attracted by her youth and her beauty, Count Thomas then married her himself, claiming that Philip II was already married (the French King had married Ingeborg of Denmark in 1193 but had repudiated her soon thereafter). Margaret's father fell sick and died after the wedding, and her mother died the following year. Margaret was the mother of either 14 or 19 children.

«b»Issue«/b»
The children of Marguerite and Thomas I of Savoy were:

1.) Amadeus IV of Savoy (1197-1253)

2.) Helena of Savoy (d. 1230)

3.) Humbert (d. 1223)

4.) Thomas, Count of Flanders, count in Piedmont

5.) Elisabeth of Savoy (d. 1235)

6.) Aimone (d. 1237), Lord of Chablais

7.) Henry (1205-1230), Lord of Lyon

8.) William of Savoy, Bishop of Valence and Dean of Vienne

9.) Amadeus of Savoy, Bishop of Maurienne

10.) Mary of Savoy (d. 1210)

11.) Magdalena of Savoy (d. 1239), abbess of Hautecombe Abbey

12.) Peter II of Savoy, Earl of Richmond and later disputed count of Savoy

13.) Philip I of Savoy, archbishop of Lyon, later Count Palatine of Burgundy by marriage and disputed count of Savoy in 1268

14.) Boniface of Savoy, Archbishop of Canterbury

15.) Beatrice of Savoy (1205-4 January 1267), wife of Ramon Berenguer IV, Count of Provence. She was married in 1219 and was mother to four queens consort and maternal grandmother of Philip III of France and Edward I of England.

16.) Alasia of Savoy (d. 1250), abbess of St Pierre, Lyon

17.) Agatha of Savoy (d. 1245), abbess of St Pierre, Lyon

18.) Margaret of Savoy (d. 1273), wife of Hartmann I of Kyburg

19.) Avita of Savoy (1215-92), wife of Baldwin de Redvers, 7th Earl of Devon and later of Robert Aguillon (d. 1286)

Margaret was the ancestress of many royal houses, including the Valois and Bourbons. After her death, she was buried at Hautecombe Abbey in Savoy.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Marguerite de Genève於1180年1月1日出生在瑞士吉艾福e-布格利斯時，她的父親William de Geneve I50歲，母親Beatrix de Faucigny22歲。她於1195年5月與Thomas de Savoie I在法國薩伏伊結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1257年4月8日在法國o-阿爾培斯易瑟艾皮-a特爾享年77歲，埋葬在法國o-阿爾培斯薩伏伊沙吉皮-德-爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Genève
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1 Altered spelling of northern French Denef(v)e, a habitational name, with the preposition de, for someone from places called Neffe in the Belgian provinces of Namur and Luxembourg.
2 Dutch (de Neve): kinship name from Middle Dutch neve ‘grandson’, ‘(male) cousin’, ‘nephew’ + the definite article de.

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: When Marguerite de Genève was born on 1 January 1180, in Chêne-Bougeries, Geneva, Switzerland, her father, William de Geneve I, was 50 and her mother, Beatrix de Faucigny, was 22. She married Thomas de Savoie I in May 1195, in Savoie, France. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 8 April 1257, in Pierre-Châtel, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, France, at the age of 77, and was buried in Saint-Pierre-de-Curtille, Savoie, Rhône-Alpes, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marguerite de Genève est née le 1 janvier 1180, à Chêne-Bougeries, Genève, Suisse, son père, William de Geneve I, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Beatrix de Faucigny, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Savoie I en mai 1195, à Savoie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 avril 1257, à Pierre-Châtel, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, France, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint-Pierre-de-Curtille, Savoie, Rhône-Alpes, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marguerite de Genève am 1. Januar 1180 in Chêne-Bougeries, Genf, Schweiz geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Geneve I 50 und ihre Mutter Beatrix de Faucigny 22. Sie hat im Mai 1195 in Savoyen, Frankreich Thomas de Savoie I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. April 1257 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Pierre-Châtel, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich und wurde in Saint-Pierre-de-Curtille, Savoie, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite de Genève è nata il il 1 gennaio 1180 nel Chêne-Bougeries, Ginevra, Svizzera, sua padre, William de Geneve I, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Beatrix de Faucigny, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Savoie I nel maggio 1195, nel Savoia, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 8 aprile 1257, nel Pierre-Châtel, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolta a Saint-Pierre-de-Curtille, Savoie, Rhône-Alpes, Francia.

ko.Le Pra, Alpes-Maritimes, Provence-Alpes-: 프랑스 플로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 알페스-마이팀스 일 플라
ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Genève(이)가 1180년 1월 1일 스위스 e브 che니-보우그리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Geneve I(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrix de Faucigny(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1195년 5월 프랑스 서아보이에서 Thomas de Savoie I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1257년 4월 8일 프랑스 ho니-알프스 이스e르 피레-cha텔에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 ho니-알프스 서아보이 세인트-피레-데-커틸에 묻혔다.

pt.1 January 1180–8 April 1257: 1 de janeiro de 1180–8 de abril de 1257
pt.Le Pra, Alpes-Maritimes, Provence-Alpes-: Le Pra, Alpes-Maritimes, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite de Genève nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1180, em Chêne-Bougeries, Genève, Suiça, seu pai, William de Geneve I, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix de Faucigny, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Savoie I em maio de 1195, em Savoie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de abril de 1257, em Pierre-Châtel, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, França, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint-Pierre-de-Curtille, Savoie, Rhône-Alpes, França.

ru.Le Pra, Alpes-Maritimes, Provence-Alpes-: л пра, алпес-маритимесь, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marguerite de Genève родилась 1 января 1180 г. в местности чeне-боугириесь, дженeв, Швейцария, её отцу по имени William de Geneve I было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Faucigny, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Savoie I май 1195 г. в местности савои, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 8 апреля 1257 г. в местности пьирре-чaтел, исeр, рoне-алпесь, Франция в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт-пьирре-де-куртилл, савои, рoне-алпесь, Франция.

es.Le Pra, Alpes-Maritimes, Provence-Alpes-: Le Pra, Alpes-Maritimes, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marguerite de Genève nació el 1 de enero de 1180, en Chêne-Bougeries, Genève, Suiza, su papá, William de Geneve I, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Beatrix de Faucigny, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Thomas de Savoie I en mayo de 1195, en Savoie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 8 de abril de 1257, en Pierre-Châtel, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Saint-Pierre-de-Curtille, Savoie, Rhône-Alpes, Francia.
